name: Source Fidelity appraisal for Tad Pde5 Class Transfer Agent Directness bundle
summary: Accepted. DailyMed source metadata and label dates support the staged tadalafil, sildenafil, vardenafil, and avanafil label comparisons; NCBI Bookshelf supports the class mechanism overview; NCBI E-utilities metadata support the Seftel and Bischoff comparative review records. The framework appropriately marks Seftel and Bischoff as abstract-level sources and carries low-priority full-text follow-ups without making unsupported fine-grained claims from them.

findings.claim_or_issue: The four label sources are located and date-stamped: CIALIS March 2026, VIAGRA January 2020, vardenafil January 2026, and STENDRA July 2024.
findings.why_it_matters: The framework depends on current or latest accessible regulatory label differences for half-life, nitrate timing, selectivity, and warnings.
findings.recommended_action: No correction required; re-check label dates during future safety or transfer updates.

findings.claim_or_issue: Seftel 2004 and Bischoff 2004 are used at abstract/metadata level in this first baseline.
findings.why_it_matters: Abstract-level extraction is adequate for broad transfer rules but should not carry detailed potency, efficacy, or adverse-effect hierarchy beyond label-supported statements.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the full-text follow-up actions open before increasing confidence in detailed review-derived transfer rules.
